description: POST /v1/spaces — unauthenticated. Creates a space plus its owner device; returns space_id, device_id, and owner_token. Matches openapi.yaml #/paths/~1v1~1spaces/post and v1-architecture.md §2.2/§10.
notes: device_id is the owner device's id, minted + persisted server-side (P0-1). The creating Mac MUST keep it: device_seq is scoped to (device_id, space), so its subsequent POST /v1/events must carry this device_id in each event body. This is the same device_id used in events-post-ack.json.

As an owner device it is a source-superset (v1-architecture.md §3): the very same owner_token that created the space can immediately POST /v1/events to report tasks/metrics — the P0-1 defect was that it previously got 403 there.

owner_token's secret segment must be exactly 48 lowercase hex characters (24 random bytes) per the sr1 grammar in v1-architecture.md §10.1 — this fixture's example is illustrative length-correct hex, not a real credential.
space_id is the non-secret locator embedded in every token minted for this space; it is also the routing key for env.SPACE_HUB.getByName(space_id).
